data.title: Healthcare Stocks Climb — Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Healthcare shares were in the green, with broad gains in insurance and life sciences.

CVS, Cigna and Humana all ended higher. The life-sciences sector finished the week on the right foot, too, with heavyweights Danaher and Thermo Fisher Scientific rising.

On a good day for pharmaceuticals, Viatris was the exception, ending in the red after posting a fourth-quarter loss Thursday evening.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: First Savings Financial Group, Inc. Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description:
JEFFERSONVILLE, Ind., Feb. 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — First Savings Financial Group, Inc. (the “Company”), the holding company for First Savings Bank (the “Bank”), announced that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.16 per common share. The dividend will be paid on or about March 31, 2025 to stockholders of record as of the close of business March 14, 2025.

The Bank is an entrepreneurial community bank headquartered in Jeffersonville, Indiana, which is directly across the Ohio River from Louisville, Kentucky, and operates fifteen depository branches within Southern Indiana. The Bank also has two national lending programs, including single-tenant net lease commercial real estate and SBA lending, with offices located predominately in the Midwest. The Bank is a recognized leader, both in its local communities and nationally for its lending programs. The employees of First Savings Bank strive daily to achieve the organization’s vision, We Expect To Be The BEST community BANK, which fuels our success. The Company’s common shares trade on The NASDAQ Stock Market under the symbol “FSFG.”

Contact:

Tony A. Schoen

Chief Financial Officer

(812) 283-0724

data.title: Financial Shares Gain to End Week — Financials Roundup
data.description:
Financial company shares marked broad, modest gains.

U.S. money-center banks and regional competitors mostly ended higher.

Consumer-finance names such as Discover Financial Services and Capital One Financial rose despite downbeat January consumer-spending data.

It was a good trading day for the insurance industry — and especially for Pennsylvania insurer Erie Indemnity, which jumped on Friday's board after posting a solid earnings report Thursday evening.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: Consumer Stocks Rise — Consumer Roundup
data.description:
A drop in consumer-spending in January didn't hurt consumer stocks, with many consumer-discretionary names rising.

The gains spread from restaurant firms such as Darden Restaurants — parent of Olive Garden — to companies including General Motors and Tesla.

Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings missed out on the cheer, falling for a second straight day despite reporting that it swung to a fourth-quarter profit on Thursday morning.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: Technology Shares Rise; Palantir Stops Skid — Tech Roundup
data.description:
Technology shares gained.

Palantir paused its brutal selloff to end the week, finishing slightly higher. It has been hit by concerns about a possible cut in U.S. military spending.

Autodesk sank after it said it was cutting 9% of its work force, part of a restructuring.

Shares of NetApp, a data-storage firm, slumped after a disappointing fiscal-third-quarter earnings report Thursday evening.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: Communication Services Companies Mark Modest Gains — Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications stocks mostly ended higher.

Online, Meta and Google ended the day with solid gains.

On screen, Warner Bros. Discovery posted a recovery, ending in the green after falling following Thursday morning's earnings report.

In mobile, AT&T climbed to lock in a double-digit percentage gain in February.

data.title: RKLB ACTIVE INVESTIGATION: Contact Levi & Korsinsky if you Lost Money on your Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Investment
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 28, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Rocket Lab USA, Inc. ("Rocket Lab USA, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On February 25, 2025, Bleecker Street Research released a report that alleges the company misled investors about the timeline for its Neutron rocket, which Rocket Lab has repeatedly claimed will launch by mid-2025. The report cites industry experts suggesting that the launch could be delayed by one to two years due to setbacks in engine development, production, and launch site readiness. It also raises concerns about Rocket Lab's financial position and the need for additional capital.

Following this news, shares of Rocket Lab fell over 10% in intraday trading. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/rocket-lab-usa-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=133180&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Synopsys prepares about $10 billion bond sale to finance Ansys deal, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Synopsys is looking to sell about $10 billion of bonds as soon as next week to help finance its acquisition of software maker Ansys , Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The company asked Bank of America, HSBC Holdings and JPMorgan Chase to organise a series of fixed-income investor calls on Friday, the report stated, citing a different source.

Synopsys, JPMorgan and HSBC did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment, while Bank of America declined to comment.

Synopsys received approval for its $35 billion deal with Ansys in the European Union (EU) last month.

To alleviate competition concerns arising from the deal, the EU said that both companies have agreed to divest Synopsys' optics and photonics software and Ansys' PowerArtist software.

The company has begun discussions with Chinese regulators about the deal, Synopsys Chief Executive Sassine Ghazi told Reuters on Wednesday.

The acquisition, announced in January last year, would see Synopsys take over Ansys, a maker of software used in the creation of a diverse range of products, including airplanes and tennis rackets used by players such as Novak Djokovic.

The potential bond sale comes after Synopsys issued a robust revenue forecast for the second quarter on Wednesday, on the back of growing demand for its software used in designing chips.

data.title: Trump compares UK's demand for Apple user data to Chinese monitoring
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump likened the UK government's demand that Apple grant it access to some user data as "something that you hear about with China," in an interview with The Spectator political magazine published Friday.

Trump said that he had told British Prime Minister Keir Starmer that he "can't do this", referring to the request for access to data. The two met at the White House on Thursday for the first time since the U.S. leader took office, discussing Ukraine and negotiating a bilateral trade agreement.

"We actually told him (Starmer) ... that's incredible. That's something, you know, that you hear about with China," Trump said in his first magazine interview of his second term with the magazine's editor-at-large Ben Domenech.

A spokesperson for the British government said "we have a close intelligence relationship with the U.S. and we take the partnership seriously" but did not comment on the specifics of the Apple case. The company did not respond to a Reuters request for comment.

Apple last week ended an advanced security encryption feature for cloud data for UK users in an unprecedented response to government demands for access to user data. A spokesperson for Britain's Home Office had then declined to comment on whether such an order had been issued.

In a letter dated February 25 to two U.S. lawmakers, Tulsi Gabbard, the U.S. director of national intelligence, said the U.S. is examining whether the UK government had violated the CLOUD Act, which bars it from issuing demands for the data of U.S. citizens and vice versa.

The Spectator, which is influential in Conservative circles and was previously edited by former Prime Minister Boris Johnson, was bought last year by British hedge fund founder Paul Marshall.

data.title: Spirit AeroSystems reports $577 million quarterly operating loss
data.description:
Spirit AeroSystems reported a $577 million fourth-quarter operating loss on Friday compared with $215 million in operating income a year earlier, which was helped by a funding agreement with key customer Boeing .

The major supplier to the U.S. planemaker reported steep losses for 2024 after warning in November there was "substantial doubt" it would be able to continue as a going concern.

Boeing is in the process of acquiring its Wichita-based former subsidiary in a deal expected to close this year.

Spirit Aero reported free cash flow, a metric closely watched by investors, of $91 million during the quarter compared with $42 million a year earlier. Revenue declined 9% to $1.65 billion during the quarter.

Spirit, which produces aerostructures parts for both Boeing and rival Airbus , said its deliveries were up on models for both planemakers during the quarter.

“Deliveries were up twofold on the 737, 37% on the A220 and 15% on the A350 compared to the prior quarter,” said Chief Financial Officer Irene Esteves in a statement.

“We believe this progress demonstrates that, with the right customer support, we are able to meet current demands while also investing for future production rate increases.”

data.title: Trump's 'World War III' warning to Zelensky rattled stocks. Why they quickly recovered.
data.description:
By Joy Wiltermuth

Markets are reacting like it's 'theater' and a Ukraine-Russia peace deal can still move forward, strategist says

U.S. stocks were largely looking past a heated discussion on Friday between U.S. President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the Oval Office, which prompted Trump to claim Ukraine was gambling with "World War III."

After an initial dip for stocks and a brief spike in Wall Street's "fear gauge," equities were moving higher - reacting to the exchange as more "theater" from Trump amid hopes that a Ukraine-Russia peace deal could still move forward, said Keith Lerner, co-chief investment officer at Truist Advisory Services, on Friday afternoon.

"Rightly or wrongly," Lerner said, the reaction from U.S. and European stocks suggests that "ultimately a deal still gets done."

Stocks briefly fell after the Trump-Zelensky exchange, which occured during a meeting aimed to further a Ukraine-U.S. minerals deal and to promote a Russia-Ukraine peace deal. Tempers flared, instead, and the meeting was cut short. Yet stocks then turned higher in the afternoon session.

"This is a very emotional time," said Richard Steinberg, chief market strategist at Focus Partners Wealth, who cautioned investors against making any rash moves in the market.

It has been an ugly February for the stock market, as some of the popular "Trump trades" from November's election have faded, consumer sentiment has soured, bitcoin (BTCUSD) and megacap tech stocks MAGS have stumbled and talk of a potential correction in equities has taken hold.

For the month, the S&P 500 SPX was on pace for a 2.4% drop, the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA was 2.4% lower and the Nasdaq Composite COMP was down almost 5.1%, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Earlier this week, Lerner's team downgraded equities to "neutral" from "attractive," on a more mixed outlook for the U.S. economy and forward corporate earnings that have "flatlined" - losing momentum while valuations remain high.

The 10-year Treasury yield BX:TMUBMUSD10Y has dramatically eased back from its recent 4.8% peak, trading at 4.23% on Friday and potentially signaling concerns about the U.S. economy.

-Joy Wiltermuth

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Perma-Pipe Intl Holdings, Inc. Announces the Appointment of Jon Biro to Its Bd of Directors
DelphX Announces Grant of Stock Options
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - February 28, 2025) - DelphX Capital Markets Inc. (OTCQB: DPXCF) ("DelphX"), a leader in the development of new classes of structured products for the fixed income market, announces that its Board of Directors has approved the grant of 950,000 stock options (the "Options") to eligible participants under its stock option plan (the "Plan"). The Options have a two-year maturity and are exercisable for common shares of DelphX at an exercise price of $0.20 per common share, all in accordance with the Plan.

About DelphX Capital Markets Inc.

DelphX is a technology and financial services company focused on developing and distributing the next generation of structured products. Through its special purpose vehicle Quantem LLC, the Company enables fixed income dealers to offer new private placement securities that provide mitigation of spread and capital charge losses when downgrades occur, while allowing for attractive returns. The new DelphX securities will enable dealers and their qualified institutional investors (QIBs) accounts to competitively structure, sell and make markets in:

  • Collateralized put options (CPOs) that provide secured rating downgrade protection for underlying corporate bonds;
  • Collateralized reference notes (CRNs) that enable investors to take on a capped rating downgrade exposure of an underlying security in exchange for attractive returns.

All CPOs and CRNs are fully collateralized and held in custody by BNY Mellon. CPOs and CRNs are proprietary products created and owned by DelphX Capital Markets.

For more information about DelphX, please visit www.delphx.com

George Wentworth, General Manager

DelphX Capital Markets Inc.

george.wentworth@delphx.com

(718) 509-2160

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/242951

data.title: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. (ESRT), a New York City-focused real estate investment trust (REIT), has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. ESRT's portfolio includes office, retail, and multifamily assets, with the Empire State Building and its world-famous Observatory as the flagship property.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $767.9 million, reflecting a 3.8% increase from the previous year driven by higher rental and observatory revenues.
  • Operating Income: $158.7 million, an 8.2% increase due to higher revenues and controlled operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $80.4 million, a slight decrease of 4.8% compared to the previous year, impacted by lower gains on property dispositions.
  • Net Income Attributable to Common Shareholders: $47.4 million, reflecting a 3.3% decrease from the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. (ESRT) is a NYC-focused real estate investment trust (REIT) that owns and operates a portfolio of office, retail, and multifamily assets. The company's flagship property is the Empire State Building, which includes the world-famous Observatory.
  • Portfolio Composition: As of December 31, 2024, ESRT's portfolio comprised approximately 7.8 million rentable square feet of office space, 0.8 million rentable square feet of retail space, and 732 residential units.
  • Leasing Activity: In 2024, ESRT signed a total of 1,324,824 rentable square feet of new, renewal, and expansion leases, contributing to a 130 basis point increase in Manhattan office occupancy to 89.0% and a 160 basis point increase in the leased rate to 94.2%.
  • Observatory Performance: The Empire State Building Observatory had approximately 2.6 million visitors in 2024, maintaining its status as the #1 Attraction in the World and the U.S. according to Tripadvisor’s 2024 Travelers’ Choice Awards.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: ESRT is recognized as a leader in sustainability, focusing on net zero emissions, energy efficiency, and indoor environmental quality. The company has published the Empire Building Playbook to guide other buildings in achieving net zero carbon reduction.
  • Geographical Performance: The majority of ESRT's office properties are located in midtown Manhattan, with one property in Stamford, Connecticut. The company has disposed of non-core assets in the greater New York metropolitan area, including properties in Stamford, Norwalk, White Plains, and Harrison.
  • Future Outlook: ESRT intends to focus its acquisition strategy on NYC office, retail, and multifamily properties to achieve attractive returns on invested capital. The company also plans to enhance shareholder value through leasing, Observatory ticket sales, and sustainability goals.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company executed a consensual foreclosure of the First Stamford Place property, which was placed in receivership, and reclassified related debt. Additionally, the company closed on a $715 million unsecured credit agreement, consisting of a $620 million revolver and a $95 million term loan facility, maturing in 2029. The company also issued $225 million in senior unsecured notes, including Series I, J, and K Green Guaranteed Senior Notes, to support its strategic initiatives.
  • Capital Management: The company maintained a strong liquidity position with $385.5 million in cash and cash equivalents and $500 million available under its unsecured revolving credit facility. It declared dividends of $0.035 per share for each quarter of 2024, equating to an annualized rate of $0.14 per share. The Board authorized a $500 million stock and operating partnership unit repurchase program, with no repurchases made during 2024. The company also refinanced the Metro Center property mortgage, securing a $71.6 million interest-only loan with a four-year term.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to meet its short-term liquidity needs through cash flows from operations, cash on hand, and available borrowing capacity. For long-term capital requirements, it will rely on cash flows, credit facilities, mortgage financings, and potential equity issuances. The company aims to maintain its REIT status by distributing at least 90% of its taxable income and will continue to evaluate its dividend and capital allocation policies. The company is focused on leveraging its capital structure to support growth and acquisition opportunities while managing its debt obligations.

Challenges and Risks

  • Geographic Concentration: ESRT faces significant challenges due to its geographic concentration in New York City and Stamford, Connecticut. This concentration exposes the company to local economic and regulatory risks, such as business layoffs, industry slowdowns, and increased local taxes. The company's reliance on the Empire State Building and its Observatory for a substantial portion of revenue further heightens this risk.
  • Real Estate Market Risks: The real estate market presents challenges such as adverse economic and geopolitical conditions, which could reduce demand and rental rates for office, multifamily, and retail spaces. The COVID-19 pandemic has already impacted these areas, and future public health crises or economic downturns could further affect property demand and value. The shift towards remote work could also reduce demand for office space.
  • Tenant Financial Stability: ESRT faces risks related to tenant financial stability, as the bankruptcy or insolvency of tenants could lead to lease terminations and material losses. The competitive leasing environment in New York City may require the company to offer concessions or make significant capital expenditures to attract and retain tenants.
  • Ground Leases and Development Risks: The company is exposed to risks from its ground leases, which could result in loss of property use if leases are breached or not renewed. Development activities also pose risks, including construction delays and cost overruns.
  • Inflation and Debt Levels: Significant inflation could increase property and development costs, potentially reducing profit margins if rental rates cannot be raised accordingly. Additionally, ESRT's debt levels and associated restrictions could limit financial flexibility and impact cash flow.
  • Market Risks: ESRT is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could affect the cost of debt and refinancing capabilities. The company's reliance on external capital sources for growth, due to REIT distribution requirements, adds to this risk. Additionally, natural disasters and climate risks could impact property values and operations, particularly given the concentration of properties in the New York metropolitan area.

SEC Filing: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. [ ESRT ] - 10-K - Feb. 28, 2025

data.title: ENTERPRISE FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Enterprise Financial Services Corp, a diversified financial services company, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company, which operates primarily in Arizona, California, Florida, Kansas, Missouri, Nevada, and New Mexico, focuses on providing a broad range of business and personal banking services, including wealth management and specialized lending niches.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $851.1 million, reflecting an increase from $764.9 million in 2023 due to higher average loan balances and increased loan yields.
  • Total Interest Expense: $283.0 million, up from $202.3 million in 2023, primarily due to increased deposit interest expense.
  • Net Interest Income: $568.1 million, a slight increase from $562.6 million in 2023, driven by higher interest income offset by increased interest expense.
  • Net Income: $185.3 million, a decrease from $194.1 million in 2023, impacted by higher noninterest expenses and increased deposit costs.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $4.86, down from $5.09 in 2023, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $4.83, compared to $5.07 in 2023, consistent with the decline in net income.
  • Return on Average Assets: 1.25%, down from 1.41% in 2023, indicating a decrease in profitability relative to asset size.
  • Return on Average Common Equity: 10.60%, a decrease from 12.27% in 2023, reflecting lower net income.
  • Net Interest Margin (fully tax equivalent): 4.16%, down from 4.43% in 2023, primarily due to higher interest expense on deposits.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company offers a broad range of business and personal banking services, including wealth management, commercial and industrial loans, commercial real estate, real estate construction and development, residential real estate, and specialty loans. The focus is on privately-held businesses, their owner families, and success-minded individuals.
  • Geographical Performance: Enterprise Financial Services Corp operates primarily in Arizona, California, Florida, Kansas, Missouri, Nevada, and New Mexico, with loan and deposit production offices throughout the United States. The company leverages its geographical presence to maximize growth opportunities.
  • Sales Units: The company has a diversified loan portfolio with significant contributions from commercial and industrial loans, commercial real estate loans, and construction and land development loans. The focus on specialized lending niches such as SBA 7(a) loans, life insurance premium finance, sponsor finance, and tax credit-related lending has contributed to loan growth.
  • New Product Launches: The company has developed expertise in niche markets, including SBA 7(a) loans, life insurance premium finance, sponsor finance, and tax credit-related lending, which differentiate it from competitors.
  • New Production Launches: In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company successfully completed the conversion of its legacy core system into a new core banking platform, enhancing operational efficiency and customer service capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on building long-term client relationships, expanding its specialized lending activities, and leveraging technology to enhance service delivery. It also aims to participate in future NMTC allocations and maintain disciplined growth through acquisitions.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Core System Conversion: In 2024, the company successfully completed the conversion of its legacy core system into a new core banking platform, which is expected to enhance operational efficiency and customer service capabilities.
  • Loan Origination: The company focused on originating high-quality commercial and industrial (C&I) loan relationships, leveraging specialized products such as sponsor finance, life insurance premium financing, and tax credit lending to expand and diversify its geographic concentration.
  • Capital Management: The company increased its tangible book value per share by 10.1% in 2024, driven by a return on average assets of 1.25%. It repurchased 626,778 of its common shares at a weighted-average share price of $46.95, and paid dividends of $1.06 per share, a 6% increase from the previous year. The company maintained strong capital ratios, with a common equity tier 1 capital ratio of 11.8% and a total capital to risk-weighted assets ratio of 14.6%. The company also had a revolving line of credit for an aggregate amount up to $25 million, all of which was available at the end of 2024.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on strategic loan growth and capital management, with an emphasis on maintaining strong capital ratios and liquidity. It aims to leverage its new core banking platform to enhance customer service and operational efficiency. The company also intends to explore further opportunities for capital investment, particularly in areas that align with its strategic focus on high-quality C&I loan relationships and specialized financial products.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive Market: The company operates in highly competitive markets, facing competition from large financial institutions and non-bank entities, which could impact its ability to attract and retain customers. The company's growth strategy relies on acquisitions, which present integration risks and potential exposure to unknown liabilities.
  • Economic and Market Conditions: The company faces significant risks related to general economic and market conditions, including potential adverse effects from economic downturns, changes in monetary and fiscal policies, and trade policy uncertainties. The banking industry has experienced volatility due to high-profile bank failures, which could erode customer confidence and impact liquidity and stock prices.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks include changes in government regulation, compliance with the Bank Secrecy Act, and potential sanctions for non-compliance with fair lending laws.
  • Financial Risks: Financial risks involve interest rate fluctuations, credit losses, and impairments to goodwill. The company also faces credit and liquidity risks due to concentration in certain markets and reliance on brokered deposits.
  • Technological and Cybersecurity Risks: Technological and cybersecurity risks are significant, with potential operational disruptions and reputational damage from cyber-attacks.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to maintain asset quality and manage expenses as critical to sustaining growth and profitability. The company is focused on building long-term client relationships and leveraging specialized lending niches to differentiate itself in the market. Strategic acquisitions are a key component of the company's growth strategy, but they require careful integration to achieve expected benefits.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to interest rate risk, which could affect its net interest margin and financial performance. It uses derivative transactions to manage interest rate risk, but these transactions introduce additional credit and market risks. The company must carefully monitor these risks to mitigate potential adverse effects on its business and financial condition.

SEC Filing: ENTERPRISE FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP [ EFSC ] - 10-K - Feb. 28, 2025

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​
